data.body:
## Description

Write an integration test that verifies two sequential worktree merges each produce exactly one version increment, and the final version is exactly two patch increments above the pre-merge baseline.

## Context

This test validates the core epic success criterion: 'Two worktree sessions that both modify code files can merge to main sequentially: both merges exit 0, plugin.json contains no conflict markers, and the final version is exactly two patch increments above the pre-merge baseline.'

The test uses local git repository fixtures (no network calls) to simulate two parallel worktrees merging sequentially with version bumping.

## Test Approach

Create tests/scripts/test-merge-to-main-version-bump-integration.sh:

1. Set up a local bare git repo as 'origin'
2. Clone the bare repo twice as two simulated 'worktrees' (worktree-a, worktree-b)
3. In each worktree, create a version file (plugin.json with version 0.1.0)
4. Simulate worktree-a merging: call _phase_version_bump equivalent with a real bump-version.sh call
   - Expected: version becomes 0.1.1
5. Simulate worktree-b merging sequentially (after worktree-a's push): call bump-version.sh on the post-a version
   - Expected: version becomes 0.1.2
6. Assert final version is 0.1.2 (exactly two patch increments above 0.1.0 baseline)
7. Assert no conflict markers in version file

Note: This test does NOT invoke the full merge-to-main.sh (which requires worktree git context and origin). It tests the bump-version.sh behavior in the merge-sequence scenario directly, which is the observable behavior that matters.

## TDD Requirement

The integration test MUST pass after Task 63b9-5261 (implementation) is complete. This task depends on Task 63b9-5261.

No RED test is required for this task (per Integration Test Task Rule: the integration test may be written after implementation, as it verifies end-to-end boundary behavior rather than driving implementation).

## File Paths

- Create: tests/scripts/test-merge-to-main-version-bump-integration.sh (executable)
- Uses: plugins/dso/scripts/bump-version.sh, plugins/dso/scripts/read-config.sh
- Does NOT require: network access, full merge-to-main.sh invocation

## Fuzzy-Match Note

test-merge-to-main-version-bump-integration.sh normalized -> testmergetomainversionbumpintegrationsh
merge-to-main.sh normalized -> mergetomainsh
'mergetomainsh' is a substring of 'testmergetomainversionbumpintegrationsh' -> auto-detected by test gate
